name: Veal Glaze- Master Chefs
recipeIngredient: -- combination of veal and beef bones
2 cups Water, plus more as needed
2 medium Tomatoes, fresh or canned, cored and coarsely chopped
1/2 teaspoon Thyme, dried OR 3 sprigs Thyme
recipeInstructions:
If you have previously prepared Veal Stock, boil it until it reduces to about 2 tablespoons and thickens to a syrup-like consistency. If not, follow these steps:
1. Preheat oven to 450°F.
2. Place oil in a roasting pan and heat briefly in the oven.
3. Add bones to the oil in the pan, tossing to coat, and roast for 35 minutes.
4. Add onions, carrots, celery, leek, garlic, and parsley, tossing again to coat with fat. Roast for an additional 30 minutes.
5. Remove the pan from the oven and transfer bones and vegetables to a clean stockpot. Drain as much fat as possible.
6. Place the roasting pan over medium-high heat (use two burners if necessary) and add 2 cups cold water. Boil briefly, scraping up browned bits into the water.
7. Transfer liquid to the stock pot, adding enough cold water to cover ingredients. Bring slowly to a boil, skimming froth.
8. Lower heat, add tomatoes, thyme (dried or fresh sprigs), bay leaves, cloves, and salt. Simmer uncovered for 6-8 hours, adding water as necessary to keep ingredients covered. Skim whenever needed.
9. Add peppercorns during the last 15 minutes of simmering.
10. Strain "soup" through a colander lined with a double layer of dampened cheesecloth into a large bowl. Press solids gently to extract liquid, discarding solids.
11. Pour stock into containers for storage, labeling and dating them. The stock keeps up to 3 days in the refrigerator and up to 6 months in the freezer. Use this stock to make the Glaze as noted at the beginning of these directions.
